title: helianthemum polyanthum var. hirsutum
intro: Helianthemum polyanthum var. hirsutum, also known as the hairy rock rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Cistaceae family, typically found in mountainous regions. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of the hairy rock rose plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis and eczema. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is also used in traditional medicine as an ingredient in various herbal preparations to promote overall well-being.
